memory.title: Memory Providers
memory.emptyDescription: Add a memory provider to enable long-term memory for your bots
memory.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this memory provider? Bots using it will lose memory access.
memory.name: Name
memory.builtinModeDescription: Choose how the built-in memory provider stores and retrieves memory for bots.
memory.sparseInstallHint: Sparse mode depends on the optional sparse service. Enable the sparse installation option when running the installer or start the sparse profile in Docker Compose.
memory.denseEmbeddingModelDescription: Select the third-party embedding model used before local rerank.
memory.denseQdrantHint: Dense memory will use Qdrant as the storage backend after the backend runtime is connected.
memory.sparseQdrantCollectionDescription: Sparse mode writes to the Qdrant collection. Current/default collection: {collection}.
memory.denseQdrantCollectionDescription: Dense mode writes to the Qdrant collection. Current/default collection: {collection}.

modeDescriptions.off: Keep the current file-based built-in memory behavior without Qdrant retrieval.
modeDescriptions.sparse: Use the local OpenSearch sparse model for memory indexing and retrieval with Qdrant.
modeDescriptions.dense: Use the selected embedding model API to build dense vectors and store/search them directly in Qdrant.

bots.title: Bots
bots.createBotWaitHint: Creating a bot may need to pull the base image on first use. Please wait a moment after submission.
bots.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this bot?
bots.emptyDescription: Click the button above to create your first bot
bots.timezoneInheritedHint: Leave empty to inherit the user's timezone, or fall back to the system timezone.
bots.aclPresetDescription: Choose a common ACL starting point during creation. You can fine-tune it later in Access Control.
bots.aclPresetHint: Recommendation: use Private Only for personal assistants, Group Only or Group and Thread for community bots, and Allow All when you want the broadest default.
bots.aclPresetHelp: This initializes the bot ACL default effect and preset rules at creation time. You can still edit them later in Access Control.

allowAll.title: Allow All
allowAll.description: Adds no extra restriction and allows all conversation types by default.

privateOnly.title: Private Only
privateOnly.description: Defaults to deny and only allows private conversations.

groupOnly.title: Group Only
groupOnly.description: Defaults to deny and only allows group conversations.

groupAndThreadOnly.title: Group and Thread
groupAndThreadOnly.description: Defaults to deny and allows group conversations plus threads.

denyAll.title: Deny All
denyAll.description: Defaults to deny and adds no rules, so you can configure access manually later.

bots.editAvatarDescription: Set the image URL for the bot avatar.
bots.editTimezoneDescription: Set a timezone override for this bot.

checks.title: Runtime Checks
checks.subtitle: View current health status and issue details.

files.name: Name
files.previewNotAvailable: Preview not available for this file type
files.confirmDeleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

email.unbindConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this email binding?
email.subject: Subject

container.title: Container Management
container.subtitle: Manage the runtime container attached to this bot.
container.botNotReady: This bot is in lifecycle transition. Container actions are temporarily disabled.
container.empty: No container found for this bot. Create one to enable runtime tooling.
container.legacyWarning: This container uses an older architecture and needs to be recreated for full compatibility. Your data will be preserved automatically.
container.createHint: The container is created from the current image. If you explicitly enable restore, preserved data will be restored after creation.
container.createRestoreDataDescription: If a previously exported backup or legacy bind-mounted data exists, it will be restored into `/data` after the container is created.
container.createImageDescription: Docker image to use as the container base (e.g. debian:bookworm-slim, alpine:latest, ubuntu:24.04). Leave empty for the default.
container.createAdvancedDescription: Configure optional GPU access and raw CDI device mappings for this container.
container.createGpuDescription: Attach host GPU access to the new container.
container.createGpuDevicesDescription: Enter one CDI device per line or separate them with commas, for example `nvidia.com/gpu=0` or `amd.com/gpu=0`. Turning GPU off explicitly clears the saved GPU preference.
container.gpuDevicesRequired: At least one CDI device is required when GPU is enabled.
container.gpuRecreateHint: Changing GPU settings requires recreating the container. A simple start or stop will not change the devices already attached.
container.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete this container? Unpreserved data cannot be recovered.
container.deletePreserveConfirm: Are you sure you want to export `/data` and then delete this container?
container.restoreConfirm: Are you sure you want to restore preserved data into this container's `/data`?
container.rollbackConfirm: Are you sure you want to roll back to this snapshot version?
container.preservingData: Backing up data, this may take a while for large volumes...
container.restoringData: Restoring data, this may take a while for large volumes...
container.snapshotNameHint: This field is only for the user-visible display name. The internal snapshot name is generated automatically.
container.dataSubtitle: Independently manage import, export, and restore for the container `/data` directory.
container.deleteSubtitle: Before deletion, explicitly choose whether `/data` should be preserved to avoid accidental data loss.
container.preservedDataAvailable: Preserved data is available and can be restored into the current container.

settings.titleModelDescription: Select a small model to auto-generate session titles. Leave empty to disable.
settings.sparseStatusHint: Markdown files remain the only source of truth. Manual sync rebuilds Qdrant from entries under /data/memory.
settings.denseStatusHint: Markdown files remain the only source of truth. Manual sync rebuilds the dense Qdrant index from entries under /data/memory.
settings.mem0StatusHint: Markdown files remain the only source of truth. Manual sync rebuilds managed Mem0 memories from entries under /data/memory.
settings.indexedMemoryStatusPendingSave: Save the selected memory provider before viewing indexed memory status or running a sync.
settings.memorySyncSuccess: Sync completed: {fsCount} source entries, {restoredCount} restored, {storageCount} indexed.
settings.imageModelDescription: Model used for the generate_image tool. Must support image-output compatibility.
settings.heartbeatDescription: Periodically trigger agent to check for items that need attention
settings.heartbeatModelDescription: Select a model for heartbeat checks. Defaults to the bot's chat model if not set.
settings.compactionDescription: Automatically summarize older messages when context gets too large
settings.compactionRatioDescription: Percentage of older messages to compact. The remaining recent messages are kept intact.
settings.compactionModelDescription: Select a model for summarization. Defaults to the bot's chat model if not set.
settings.allowGuestPersonalHint: ACL rules apply to all bot types.
settings.deleteBotDescription: Deleting this bot cannot be undone. Proceed with caution.

access.title: Access Control
access.subtitle: Priority-ordered rules control who can trigger this bot's chat. The first matching rule wins; when no rule matches, the default effect applies.
access.defaultEffectDescription: Applied when no rule matches an inbound message.
access.rulesDescription: Drag by the handle to reorder. Lower numbers are evaluated first; the first matching rule's effect is applied.
access.rulesEmptyDescription: Add a rule to control access beyond the default effect.
access.selectIdentity: Search and select a platform identity
access.deleteConfirmDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this rule?
access.sourceScopeDescription: Optionally restrict this rule to a specific platform, conversation type, or conversation/thread.
access.conversationSourceDescription: Search by name, platform, or conversation ID. Lists group/thread routes this bot has seen — either for the selected identity, or for the selected platform type.
access.noObservedConversations: No matching conversations in history yet. Use manual IDs below, or ensure this bot has messages in those chats (including private).
access.manualConversationIdsHint: If the conversation is not listed, enter the raw conversation ID and thread ID from the platform.
access.pickIdentityForConversationSearch: Choose a platform identity above to search conversations observed for this bot.
access.pickChannelTypeForConversationSearch: Enter or choose a platform type above to search conversations this bot has seen on that platform.
access.conversationIdManualHint: Manual entry only. Search requires a platform identity.

channels.title: Platforms
channels.emptyDescription: Click below to add an access platform for this bot
channels.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this platform?
channels.webhookCallbackHint: Use this URL as the webhook callback URL in the platform console.
channels.webhookCallbackPending: Save this platform configuration to generate the callback URL.
channels.feishuWebhookSecurityHint: For security, webhook mode requires either an Encrypt Key or a Verification Token; an unprotected public callback URL should not be exposed.
channels.feishuWebhookSecretRequired: For security, configure at least one of Encrypt Key or Verification Token.
channels.noAvailableTypes: All platform types have been configured
